relevance_system_prompt: Classify whether a paper is even slightly relevant to thermal management across chip-level, board-level, or system-level cooling. Prefer recall over precision: include borderline-but-related papers. Use both title and abstract.
analysis_system_prompt: You are writing an executive collaboration brief on thermal-management research. Keep it concise, easy to follow, and table-first. Focus on partner research focus, breakthroughs, trends, and future direction.
analysis_user_prompt_template:
Write a concise markdown report using short sections and tables. Avoid long paragraphs and generic background.
Must include:
1) Collaboration by cooling category table (chip-level, board-level, system-level).
2) Partner focus table: partner name, type (University/Company/Other), dominant cooling category, paper count.
3) Most important breakthroughs (bullet list, brief, evidence-based from methodology/category stats).
4) Future direction analysis based on observed collaborations.
5) Interesting trend highlights (year/category or method/category).
Use these category definitions exactly:
- Chip-level: Microfluidics, Thermal Interface Materials (TIMs), hotspots mitigation in 2.5D/3D ICs.
- Board-level: Integrated heat pipes, vapor chambers, PCB thermal via optimization.
- System-level: Cold-plate cooling, Two-phase immersion cooling, Rack-scale thermal management.

Input topics: {topics_json}
Relevant paper count: {relevant_count}
Tagged stats: {stats_json}
Tagged paper sample: {paper_sample_json}
batch_relevance_system_prompt: You are classifying publication relevance for thermal management research. Process a batch of papers and return only strict JSON. Be recall-oriented: include anything even slightly relevant to chip-level, board-level, or system-level thermal management/cooling.
